an7.61:1.2: at one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling among the Bhaggas at Susumāragira, in Bhesakaḷā Grove, the deer park.
an7.61:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Mahāmoggallāna was sitting nodding off among the Magadhans, in the village of Kallavāḷaputta.
an7.61:1.4: The *Bhagavā* saw with the divine eye, purified and surpassing the human, Venerable Mahāmoggallāna sitting nodding off among the Magadhans, in the village of Kallavāḷaputta.
an7.61:1.6: just as a strong man might extend a bent arm or bend an extended arm; in the same way—he disappeared from among the Bhaggas at Susumāragira, in Bhesakaḷā Grove, the deer park, and appeared among the Magadhans, in the village of Kallavāḷaputta, in front of Venerable Mahāmoggallāna.
an7.61:1.7: The *Bhagavā* sat down on the seat prepared.
an7.61:1.8: Having sat down, the *Bhagavā* said this to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna:
an7.61:10.1: Therefore, Moggallāna, you should train thus:
an7.61:10.2: ‘I will not approach families with my trunk held high.’
an7.61:10.3: This is how you should train, Moggallāna.
an7.61:10.4: If, Moggallāna, a *bhikkhu* approaches families with his trunk held high, there are, Moggallāna, duties to be done in families.
an7.61:10.5: Because of these, people do not attend to the *bhikkhu* who has arrived. There the *bhikkhu* thinks:
an7.61:10.6: ‘Now who has caused a split between me and this family? Now these people seem disaffected with me.’
an7.61:10.7: Thus from non-gain there is dejection; for one dejected, restlessness; for one restless, non-restraint; for one unrestrained, the mind [*cittaṁ*] is far from *samādhi*.
an7.61:11.1: Therefore, Moggallāna, you should train thus:
an7.61:11.3: This is how you should train, Moggallāna.
an7.61:11.4: When there is argumentative talk, Moggallāna, much talk can be expected; when there is much talk, restlessness; for one restless, non-restraint; for one unrestrained, the mind [*cittaṁ*] is far from *samādhi*.
an7.61:11.5: I do not, Moggallāna, praise association with everyone.
an7.61:11.6: Nor do I, Moggallāna, fail to praise association with everyone.
an7.61:11.7: I do not praise association with householders and renunciants, Moggallāna.
an7.61:11.8: But those lodgings that are quiet, still, with an atmosphere of solitude, secluded from people, suitable for retreat — I praise association with lodgings of such a kind.”
an7.61:12.1: When this was said, Venerable Mahāmoggallāna said this to the *Bhagavā*:
an7.61:12.2: “To what extent, *bhante*, is a *bhikkhu*, in brief, liberated through the destruction of *taṇhā*, one who has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate security from bondage, the ultimate holy life, the ultimate consummation, best among *devatās* and humans?”
an7.61:13.10: To this extent, Moggallāna, a *bhikkhu* is, in brief, liberated through the destruction of *taṇhā*, one who has reached the ultimate end, the ultimate security from bondage, the ultimate holy life, the ultimate consummation, best among *devatās* and humans.”
an7.61:13.3: And when, Moggallāna, a *bhikkhu* has heard this:
an7.61:13.5: He directly knows all *dhammas*; having directly known all *dhammas*, he fully understands all *dhammas*. Having fully understood all *dhammas*, whatever *vedanā* he feels — whether *sukha*, *dukkha*, or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*.
an7.61:13.6: He dwells contemplating impermanence [*aniccā*] in those *vedanās*, dwells contemplating fading away, dwells contemplating cessation, dwells contemplating relinquishment.
an7.61:13.7: While dwelling contemplating impermanence [*aniccā*] in those *vedanās*, dwelling contemplating fading away, dwelling contemplating cessation, dwelling contemplating relinquishment, he does not take up anything in the world.
an7.61:13.8: Not taking up, he is not agitated; being unagitated, he personally attains complete *nibbāna*.
an7.61:13.9: He understands: ‘Birth is destroyed, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done; there is no more of this state.’
an7.61:2.1: “Are you nodding off, Moggallāna? Are you nodding off, Moggallāna?”
an7.61:2.3: “Therefore, Moggallāna, when, while you dwell percipient in a certain way, that drowsiness descends on you, do not attend to that *saññā*, do not cultivate that *saññā* much.
an7.61:2.4: It is possible, Moggallāna, that while you dwell in this way that drowsiness will be abandoned.
an7.61:3.1: If that drowsiness is not abandoned while you dwell in this way, then, Moggallāna, you should think over and examine in your mind the *dhamma* as you have heard and learned it, and inspect it mentally.
an7.61:3.2: It is possible that while you dwell in this way that drowsiness will be abandoned.
an7.61:4.1: If that drowsiness is not abandoned while you dwell in this way, then, Moggallāna, you should recite in detail the *dhamma* as you have heard and learned it.
an7.61:4.2: It is possible that while you dwell in this way that drowsiness will be abandoned.
an7.61:5.1: If that drowsiness is not abandoned while you dwell in this way, then, Moggallāna, you should pull both earlobes and rub your limbs with your hand.
an7.61:5.2: It is possible that while you dwell in this way that drowsiness will be abandoned.
an7.61:6.1: If that drowsiness is not abandoned while you dwell in this way, then, Moggallāna, you should rise from your seat, rub your eyes with water, look around in all directions, and look up at the constellations and forms of the stars.
an7.61:6.2: It is possible that while you dwell in this way that drowsiness will be abandoned.
an7.61:7.1: If that drowsiness is not abandoned while you dwell in this way, then, Moggallāna, you should attend to the *saññā* of light, and establish the *saññā* of day—
an7.61:7.2: as by day, so at night; as at night, so by day.
an7.61:7.3: Thus, with an open and unobstructed mind [*cetasā*], you should develop [*bhāveyyāsi*] a mind [*cittaṁ*] full of radiance.
an7.61:7.4: It is possible that while you dwell in this way that drowsiness will be abandoned.
an7.61:8.1: If that drowsiness is not abandoned while you dwell in this way, then, Moggallāna, perceiving what is before and behind, you should establish walking meditation, with the *indriyas* turned inward and the mind not gone outward.
an7.61:8.2: It is possible that while you dwell in this way that drowsiness will be abandoned.
an7.61:9.1: If that drowsiness is not abandoned while you dwell in this way, then, Moggallāna, you should lie down on your right side in the lion’s posture, placing one foot over the other, with *sati* and full awareness [*sampajāno*], having attended to the *saññā* of getting up.
an7.61:9.2: And when you have awakened, Moggallāna, you should get up quickly:
an7.61:9.3: ‘I will not dwell devoted to the *sukha* of lying down, the *sukha* of reclining, or the *sukha* of drowsiness.’
an7.61:9.4: This is how you should train, Moggallāna.
